title: humboltia pilifera
intro: Humboldtia pilifera, also known as the Devil's Tree or Pichili, is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is native to the tropical forests of Sri Lanka and India. The plant has been found to have medicinal properties due to its high alkaloid content, particularly the presence of vincamajine, which has been shown to have anti-inflammatory, anti-bacterial, and anti-parasitic properties. The health benefits of Humboldtia pilifera include its potential to treat fever, malaria, and certain types of cancer, and it can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional folk medicine to treat a range of ailments.
